text:
REGIME — Defensive rotation dominated; financials and utilities led, tech and industrials crushed.

INDICES — VIX 30.6, elevated but down on the day. DIA +0.15%, SPY -0.33%, IJR -0.72%, QQQ -0.76%, IJH -0.79%, IWM -1.44%. Large caps outperformed small caps decisively. USO surged +4.53%, the standout cross-asset move, while VIXY fell -0.92%.

SECTORS — XLF +1.15%, XLC +0.86%, XLU +0.72%, XLRE +0.50%, XLV +0.39%, XLB +0.37%, XLP +0.12%, XLY -0.02%, XLE -0.96%, XLI -1.63%, XLK -1.86%. Top industry ETFs: KIE +1.61% and WCLD +1.36%; bottom: UNG -4.89% and WGMI -8.13%.

THE 7s — Software +2.76%, Toll Road +2.05%, Insurance +1.76%, Health +1.09%, Old Staples +1.08%, New Staples +0.39%, Bank +0.12%, Freight +0.00%, Power -0.16%, Cloud -0.17%, Housing -0.41%, Old Metals -0.82%, Energy -0.96%, Medtech -1.47%, Defense -1.70%, Industrial -2.21%, Semi -2.97%, New Metals -3.14%, Buildout -4.22%, Fab 7 -5.30%. Strongest ticker in Software 7 drove the basket; BSX was the wreckage in Medtech at -9.02%.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 46.2, Neutral. Top movers: BSX -9.02% (Medtech 7), WGMI -8.13% (industry), Fab 7 basket -5.30%. 20d momentum: best sector XLE +8.63%, worst XLI -12.46%; best basket Energy +11.23%, worst Old Metals -23.43%. BSX's -9.02% on 3.1x volume spike drove Medtech 7 to -1.47% on apparent negative clinical or earnings news.

WATCH — BSX: -9.02%, 3.1x vol spike warrants monitoring for follow-through or dead-cat setup. IWM: -1.44% today, -9.01% over 20d, approaching oversold threshold with small caps structurally lagging. UNG: -4.89% today against USO +4.53%, a stark energy divergence signaling commodity-specific stress worth tracking.
